title: Psychotria thonneri
taxonomy: Psychotria thonneri belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises over 13,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are economically important. The genus Psychotria itself contains about 1,800 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Psychotria thonneri is a species within this genus, characterized by its unique morphological features and geographical distribution. Further classification of Psychotria thonneri may involve more specific taxonomic details at the species level.

intro: Psychotria thonneri is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and research has confirmed its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, and support wound healing.
